question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that properties.isEnabled() defaults to false, causing health() to always return UP regardless of actual HelmRelease state, thereby masking missing or incorrect records exported to mirror nodes with a permanently false-healthy signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated health() calls to observe that when disabled, the monitor always emits UP status, causing operators to believe records are being exported correctly when the HelmRelease is actually degraded?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cacheInvalidateIf(v -> false) predicate, which never invalidates the cached Mono by value, so that a stale UNKNOWN or UP result persists indefinitely until the time-based expiry, hiding a real degradation in record export to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user race the cache population window (first call after expiry) to ensure a transient UP result is cached for the full cacheExpiry duration, causing the monitor to report healthy record export even when the HelmRelease has just transitioned to a failed state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorComplete() call after getHelmRelease() so that any Kubernetes API error silently completes the Mono, causing switchIfEmpty(UNKNOWN) to emit UNKNOWN instead of DOWN, masking a real record-export failure as a non-critical unknown state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the second onErrorComplete() call after getHelmReleaseReadyStatus() so that a ClassCastException or NullPointerException during status parsing silently completes the Mono, causing the health check to emit UNKNOWN and hide a genuine record-export outage?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Schedulers.newSingle('helmrelease-health-cache') thread to be starved or blocked, delaying cache refresh beyond the cacheExpiry window and causing the health indicator to serve stale UP results while records are not being exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that System.getenv('HOSTNAME') is read at runtime without validation, so that if the HOSTNAME environment variable is empty or points to a non-existent pod, the Kubernetes client throws an exception that is silently swallowed, causing the health check to return UNKNOWN and mask missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of null-checking on client.pods().withName(hostname).get() so that if the pod is not found (returns null), a NullPointerException is thrown, caught by onErrorComplete(), and the health check silently returns UNKNOWN, hiding a real record-export problem?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a null check on resource returned by client.genericKubernetesResources(RESOURCE_DEFINITION_CONTEXT).withName(release).get(), so that a 404 response returns null, causing a NullPointerException swallowed by onErrorComplete(), and the health check returns UNKNOWN, masking a real record-export outage?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a null check on status.get('conditions') so that if the HelmRelease has no conditions field, a NullPointerException is thrown, swallowed by onErrorComplete(), and the health check returns UNKNOWN, hiding a genuine record-export failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the conditions.stream().filter(...).findFirst() logic so that if the HelmRelease has no 'Ready' condition, orElse(DOWN) returns DOWN, but this DOWN is then cached for the full cacheExpiry, causing a transient misconfiguration to permanently suppress record-export health signals for up to 30 seconds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-sensitive Strings.CS.equals(condition.get('type'), 'Ready') comparison so that a HelmRelease condition with type 'ready' or 'READY' is silently skipped, causing orElse(DOWN) to fire and the health check to report DOWN even when the release is actually healthy, creating a false alarm that distracts from real record-export issues?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mapStatus() only checks 'True' for UP and 'DependencyNotReady' for UNKNOWN, so that any other non-True status (e.g., 'False' with a benign reason) maps to DOWN, causing the health check to report record-export failure even when the HelmRelease is in a transient but non-critical state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of null-checking on condition.get('status') in mapStatus() so that if the condition map has no 'status' key, a null value is compared to 'True' (returning false) and then to 'DependencyNotReady' (returning false), causing the health check to return DOWN and mask the real state of record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of null-checking on condition.get('reason') in mapStatus() so that if the condition map has no 'reason' key, a NullPointerException is thrown, swallowed by onErrorComplete(), and the health check returns UNKNOWN, hiding a real record-export degradation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static AtomicInteger RELEASE_UP shared across all instances of ReleaseHealthIndicator so that concurrent health checks from multiple threads race to set RELEASE_UP, causing the Micrometer gauge to report an inconsistent value that does not reflect the true record-export health state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that recordHealthMetric() sets RELEASE_UP to 0 for any non-UP status (including UNKNOWN), so that a transient Kubernetes API error causes the gauge to drop to 0, triggering false alerts about missing record exports when the mirror node is actually healthy?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the null check 'currentHealth != null ? currentHealth.getStatus() : Status.UNKNOWN' in recordHealthMetric() so that if the reactive pipeline emits a null Health object, RELEASE_UP is set to 0, causing the gauge to falsely indicate record-export failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: registerGauge()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that registerGauge() registers a Gauge pointing to the static RELEASE_UP AtomicInteger, so that if the gauge is scraped by an external Prometheus instance accessible without authentication, an attacker can observe the health state of record exports and time attacks to coincide with known DOWN windows?
